title: hold
summary: Control whether new plots replace or append to the current axes.

gpu_support.notes: `hold` changes plotting state only. It does not execute numeric GPU kernels.

description: `hold` controls whether subsequent plotting commands append to the current axes or replace existing plot objects. The state is tracked per axes, so different subplots can have different hold settings.
behaviors: `hold('on')` enables appending to the current axes.
`hold('off')` disables appending so later plots replace the axes contents.
`hold('all')` is currently treated the same as `hold('on')`.
Logical and numeric scalar inputs are accepted: zero means off and nonzero means on.

examples.description: Append a second line to the same axes
examples.output: % both lines remain visible on the current axes
% the new plot replaces the previous axes contents
% state1 and state2 reflect the toggled boolean state

faqs.question: Is hold state per figure or per axes?
faqs.answer: It is tracked per axes, so one subplot can be in hold mode while another is not.

faqs.question: What does `hold('all')` do?
faqs.answer: RunMat currently treats `'all'` the same as `'on'`.

faqs.question: What does the return value mean?
faqs.answer: It is the resulting boolean hold state after applying the command.

gpu_residency: `hold` only mutates plotting state and does not affect array residency.
gpu_behavior: `hold` performs no provider dispatch; it updates per-axes plotting state in the host-side registry.
